question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window=5m threshold by causing the importer to be exactly at the 5-minute lag boundary, toggling between batch and non-batch mode across staking period boundaries, causing EntityStakeRepositoryCustomImpl.createEntityStateStart() to use inconsistent endPeriodTimestamp values and producing incorrect stake_total_start for pending_reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum frequency constraint (DurationMin millis=10) to observe that at 10ms polling, the parser processes record files faster than the database can commit entity_stake updates, causing EntityStakeRepository.updated() to return false even when the stake is up-to-date, triggering redundant calculate() loops that corrupt pending_reward via repeated updateEntityStake() calls?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the balance parser (BalanceParserProperties, isEnabled()=true by default) and record parser are independent, and if the balance parser is disabled while the record parser is enabled, createEntityStateStart() will find no account_balance snapshot within the ONE_MONTH_IN_NS window, causing it to return early without populating entity_state_start, and updateEntityStake() will produce zero pending_reward for all accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit BatchProperties.maxFiles=1 (default for record parser) by causing the importer to process a record file containing a NodeStakeUpdatedEvent followed immediately by a staking_reward_transfer in the same file, and the batch flush occurs between them, causing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl to calculate pending_reward before the staking_reward_transfer updates stake_period_start, resulting in double-counting of rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the transactionTimeout (default 120s in AbstractParserProperties) to be exceeded during EntityStakeRepositoryCustomImpl.createEntityStateStart(), causing the transaction to roll back and leaving entity_state_start empty, so that updateEntityStake() produces pending_reward=0 for all accounts for that staking period, effectively stealing all unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ApplicationReadyEvent-triggered calculateOnApplicationReady() in EntityStakeCalculator by causing the importer to restart repeatedly (via processingTimeout health-check failures), each time triggering a fresh calculate() that reads entity_stake with stale endStakePeriod values, causing pending_reward to be recalculated with incorrect epoch_day boundaries and inflating rewards for certain accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.flushInterval and the lockFromConcurrentUpdates() advisory lock in EntityStakeRepository, by timing a batch flush to occur while the lock is held, causing the flush to block and the NodeStakeUpdatedEvent to be delayed, resulting in EntityStakeCalculatorImpl processing the event for the wrong epoch_day and assigning pending_reward to the wrong staking period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms default frequency of the record parser to create a high-frequency polling scenario where the parser repeatedly checks for new files, and during a staking period boundary, the rapid polling causes two NodeStakeUpdatedEvents to be processed for the same epoch_day (due to multiple node_stake rows with the same epoch_day but different consensus_timestamps), resulting in pending_reward being accumulated twice?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when the record parser is disabled (isEnabled()=false), the staking_reward_transfer table is not updated, causing the last_staking_reward_transfer CTE in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration to use stale data, and when the migration runs, it computes incorrect first_period_stake_change values that undercount the stake adjustment, inflating pending_reward for accounts that already received payouts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit BatchProperties.maxItems to cause the entity_state_start table to be populated with an incomplete set of entities (only those processed before the batch limit), causing updateEntityStake() to compute pending_reward only for a subset of stakers, leaving the remaining stakers with stale pending_reward values that are never updated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the processingTimeout to be exceeded by submitting a transaction that triggers a very large staking reward payout (many accounts in paidStakingRewards), causing insertStakingRewardTransfers() to take longer than processingTimeout, the parser to restart, and the staking_reward_transfer records to be partially committed, leaving some accounts with updated stake_period_start and others without, causing inconsistent pending_reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the REST API's pending_reward calculation logic (case when e.stake_period_start >= es.end_stake_period then 0 else es.pending_reward) by observing that when the record parser is disabled, stake_period_start is never updated after a reward payout, causing the REST API to continue returning non-zero pending_reward for accounts that have already been paid, misleading users about their actual uncollected rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.window=5m and the ONE_MONTH_IN_NS window in EntityStakeRepositoryCustomImpl.createEntityStateStart() by causing the importer to fall behind by more than one month, making the balance snapshot lookup return empty, causing createEntityStateStart() to return early and entity_state_start to be empty, resulting in updateEntityStake() producing pending_reward=0 for all stakers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency-based polling to observe the exact timing of NodeStakeUpdatedEvent processing and submit a CryptoUpdate transaction to change their staked_node_id immediately after the event is processed but before entity_state_start is populated, causing their account to be included in entity_state_start with the new staked_node_id but the old stake_period_start, resulting in incorrect pending_reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityStakeCalculator.calculateOnApplicationReady() is triggered on ApplicationReadyEvent regardless of whether the record parser isEnabled(), causing calculate() to run even when the parser is disabled, and if entity_stake is empty (fresh deployment), the calculation produces pending_reward=0 for all accounts, effectively resetting any previously accumulated rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit BatchProperties.flushInterval=2s by timing a CryptoUpdate transaction that changes decline_reward from true to false to arrive in a batch that is flushed after the NodeStakeUpdatedEvent is processed, causing the entity_state_start snapshot to capture decline_reward=true for the account, resulting in the account being excluded from pending_reward calculation for that period even though it should be eligible?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the record parser's transactionTimeout (120s) to expire during the updateEntityStake() SQL execution (which involves multiple CTEs and table operations), causing the transaction to roll back, leaving entity_stake_temp populated but entity_stake not updated, and on the next calculate() invocation, the stale entity_stake_temp data is truncated and recalculated, potentially with different balance snapshots that produce incorrect pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pending_reward field in the REST API is computed as 0 when decline_reward=true or staked_node_id=-1, and if the record parser is disabled (isEnabled()=false), a CryptoUpdate that sets decline_reward=false is never processed, causing the account to appear as decline_reward=true in the mirror node and receive 0 pending_reward even though it should be accumulating rewards on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxItems=60000 limit to cause a scenario where a batch containing a NodeStakeUpdatedEvent is split across two processing cycles, and the second cycle's createEntityStateStart() uses a different balanceSnapshotTimestamp than the first, causing the balance_change calculation to double-count crypto_transfer amounts and inflate stake_total_start, leading to inflated pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms polling frequency to observe that the importer processes record files in near-real-time, and by monitoring the /api/v1/accounts/{id} endpoint for changes in pending_reward, determine the exact timing of staking period boundaries and submit transactions to maximize their stake_total_start at the snapshot moment, effectively gaming the pending_reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between the record parser being disabled (isEnabled()=false) and the RecalculatePendingRewardMigration, where the migration uses FIRST_NONZERO_REWARD_RATE_TIMESTAMP as a hardcoded anchor, and if the parser was disabled during that period, the crypto_transfer CTE in the migration SQL will be missing transfers, causing incorrect first_period_stake_change and inflated pending_reward for accounts that had transfers during the disabled period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.queueCapacity and the @Async annotation on EntityStakeCalculator.calculate(), where a full queue causes the async thread pool to reject the calculate() invocation, silently skipping the pending_reward update for that staking period, and the skip is only logged as INFO (not an error), making it invisible to operators?
